rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE FRIDA KOPPL TRUST ONE DATED 8/5/11 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To (1) Enforce Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For The Production Of Business Records, And (2) For Protective Order Re Medical Expert Depositions; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, September 12, 2023, Line 1, PETITIONER RUDOLPH KOPPL'S Motion To (1) Enforce Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For The Production Of Business Records, And (2) For Protective Order Re Medical Expert Depositions; And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion DENIED as moot. The Court signed an order on September 1, 2023, resolving this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPARTMENT 301 DAILY AT 9:00 A.M.), or dial the corresponding number and use the meeting ID, and password for Department 301. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANNE S. OUTIN REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST UNDER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/30/2023 to be adjudicated with the related accounting petition filed in PES-04-286107. The following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) Direct notice must continue to be given to Royal National Institute as personal representative of the sole beneficiary's estate, until a directive signed by Royal National Institute stating that notice to their attorney is sufficient is filed. See LRSF 14.14(G). 3) An original statement of each financial account listed on Schedules E & F must be filed to verify the ending balances (LRSF 14.34(D)), together with a declaration re retention of the originals required by LRSF 14.59(A). 4) Should this be the petitioner's final account upon termination of the trust with a petition for final distribution to the petitioner as personal representative of the settlor's probate estate? i) If so, the petition must be amended and renoticed. ii) If no, please explain why the administration of this trust must continue and state when final distribution to the settlor's probate estate is anticipated.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL ADOLFF JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/10/2023. 1) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 2) Copies of the DE-121 form and remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be served at least 15 days before the hearing. New notice must be given. 3) Item 2(d)(2) of the petition: i) A bond waiver signed by a successor personal representative of the estate of the decedent's postdeceased spouse must also be filed. ii) A declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to state the total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate. 4) Item 8: i) All known heirs of the postdeceased mother of the decedent must be identified and receive notice. ii) All known heirs of the postdeceased spouse of the decedent must be identified and receive notice.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM MICHAEL HENRY REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Court Approval Of Settlement Agreement And Retention Of Jurisdiction To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to review the settlement agreement for approval. It is unclear whether the petitioner re-filed the Heggstad petition (set for 10/24/2023) for the sole purpose of re-creating "pending litigation" required by CCP section 664.6 for the Court to retain jurisdiction to enforce the settlement (after the petition is removed from the Court's calendar and may be deemed no longer pending before the Court), or for the purpose of requesting the Court's order confirming the settlor's real property and bank accounts as assets of the subject trust. If the Heggstad relief remains necessary, the relief cannnot be granted on this settlement petition and must be addressed at the 10/24/2023 hearing on the Heggstad petition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILSON PENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared since June 2023. (1) Further allegation if the request for funeral expenses of $48,851 paid by aunt is or is not verified claim and its reasonableness. Administrator with full IAEA is seeking the court's approval for said reimbursement in Paragraph #20; however, the court has no further information such as the reasonableness and verified/non-verified claim. Prayer does not include the request, either. Need clarification. (2) Need allegation that the estate funds are fully covered by FDIC. Appears cash funds in on account exceeds $250K. (3) Proposed Order to be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CELIA R. JERLABAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/04/2023, as the following issues remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 05/17/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received - in light of the related civil case (quiet title action), distribution must be ordered subject to the plaintiff's interest in the real property to be determined in that action. 2) A waiver of account signed by the petitioner in his capacity as trustee of the devisee trust must be filed with his acceptance of the trust. See Probate Code section 10954(b)(4). 3) The attorney's statutory compensation and costs must be ordered to be paid out of the estate under section 10831(d). An allegation must be made as to whether the petitioner has advanced sufficient cash to the estate for the payment out of the estate. An appearance may be required on 10/04/2023 if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the outstanding issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YAN PAL HUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/04/2023, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/02/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 3) An amended DE-147 form must be filed to provide the petitioner's telephone number at item 2 of the acknowledgment of receipt section. 4) Re the DE-121 form: i) Item 1 must specify all names (aka) by which the decedent was known. See the introductory paragraph of the will for two other names used by the decedent. ii) Notice must given to the alternate executors named in the will. 5) Re the proof of publication: All names (aka) by which the decedent was known must be published. See the introductory paragraph of the will for two other names used by the decedent. 6) Item 3d: The petitioner appears to be the sole beneficiary under the will. Please explain why the petitioner is not proceeding with a spousal property petition. Note: If the petitioner intends to make an election under Probate Code section 13502, the election must be filed within 4 months after letters are issued and must include language required by LRSF 14.35(I)(5) ("statement must clearly state that she has been fully informed about the reasons for a probate (rather than, e.g., a Spousal Property Petition) and the consequences thereof, including potential delay and increased fees.") 7) Item 3f(2): The will appears to be self-proving, but the box was not checked. 8) Item 4 must be checked. 9) Item 8 must list the alternate executors named in the will and their address and relationship to the decedent.
